watering_advice: Water when top 2 cm of soil feels dry. Tomatoes prefer consistent moisture but not waterlogged soil. Water at the base to avoid leaf disease.
fertilizer_advice: Use balanced NPK fertilizer (e.g., 10-10-10) once every 2 weeks. Tomatoes need adequate potassium for fruit development.
soil_advice: Well-draining loamy soil with compost is recommended. Add organic matter to improve water retention and nutrient content.
sunlight_advice: Provide 6 to 8 hours of sunlight daily. Tomatoes are sun-loving plants and need sufficient light for proper fruit development.
care_tips: Provide sturdy support or stakes for the plant
Prune suckers to direct energy to fruit production
Ensure proper air circulation to reduce disease risk
Monitor for common pests like tomato hornworms
warnings: Early blight detected - fungal infection present
Increase air circulation around affected leaves
Avoid overhead watering to prevent disease spread
next_steps: Remove lower infected leaves to improve air flow
Monitor plant daily for disease progression
